name: docusaurus-advanced
description: Deep dive into the Docusaurus configuration, plugins, and custom fields.
capabilities: Explain Docusaurus configuration structure and best practices
Guide plugin and preset selection and setup
Assist with theme swizzling and custom CSS overrides
Provide guidance on MDX component integration
Help configure custom fields and API exposure
Support sidebar organization and navigation design
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation guide containing only informational content about Docusaurus configuration. The static analysis flagged 34 pattern-based findings, but evaluation confirms ALL are false positives: the 'hardcoded URLs' are repository metadata, 'C2 keywords' refer to content hash fields, 'weak cryptographic algorithms' are SHA-256 integrity hashes, and 'shell backticks' are documentation file path references. No executable code, command execution, or credential exfiltration exists.
valueStatement: Documentation teams struggle to customize Docusaurus sites beyond basic setup. This skill provides deep guidance on plugins, theme customization, and MDX integration for advanced documentation sites.
limitations: Does not modify files or execute Docusaurus build commands
Does not provide debugging support for build errors
Cannot generate complete documentation sites from scratch

useCases.title: Customize Documentation Sites
useCases.description: Learn to theme and brand Docusaurus sites with custom CSS and layout modifications.

useCases.title: Add Interactive Components
useCases.description: Integrate MDX components and custom plugins to enhance documentation with interactive elements.

useCases.title: Configure Build Pipeline
useCases.description: Set up API integrations, environment variables, and deployment configurations for documentation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Configuration Help
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the structure of docusaurus.config.ts and how to configure the basic settings for a documentation site.

promptTemplates.title: Plugin Selection Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: What plugins and presets are available in Docusaurus and how do I configure the classic preset for docs, blog, and pages?

promptTemplates.title: Theme Customization
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I customize the Docusaurus theme using CSS overrides and theme swizzling? What files should I modify?

promptTemplates.title: MDX Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use MDX to create interactive documentation components in Docusaurus? Provide examples of embedded React components.

bestPractices: Use the classic preset for standard documentation sites with docs, blog, and pages
Keep custom CSS in textbook/src/css/custom.css for clean theme overrides
Define sidebars in sidebars.ts for consistent navigation across your site
antiPatterns: Avoid modifying Docusaurus source files directly; use theme swizzling instead
Do not hardcode API URLs; use environment variables for different deployments
Avoid skipping sidebar configuration; it leads to inconsistent navigation

faq.question: What versions of Docusaurus are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers Docusaurus v2 and v3 configurations. Core concepts apply across versions.

faq.question: Can this skill generate a complete Docusaurus site?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides configuration guidance but cannot create files or run commands.

faq.question: How do I add custom fields to my configuration?
faq.answer: Add customFields to your docusaurus.config.ts. These are exposed to client components.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads information and provides guidance. No files are modified.

faq.question: Why is my build failing after configuration changes?
faq.answer: Check config syntax, verify plugin compatibility, and ensure required dependencies are installed.

faq.question: How does this compare to the basic Docusaurus skill?
faq.answer: The advanced skill covers plugins, theme swizzling, and MDX. The basic skill covers initial setup.
